rationale: The cited paper appears highly relevant to the citing paper’s core question: both study proactive churn interventions in telecom using randomized field experiments, and the cited work’s backfire/heterogeneity findings directly align with the citing paper’s focus on who should be targeted (RISK vs LIFT). The citing note explicitly positions itself against standard high-risk targeting in churn management, which the cited note also examines via causal campaign effects and targeting implications, so this is plausibly substantive rather than a generic mention.
